name: Golden Temple
desc: The holiest Sikh shrine with a stunning golden facade reflecting in the sacred Amrit Sarovar pool.

name: Kerala Backwaters
desc: A serene network of canals and lakes best explored on a traditional houseboat at sunset.

name: Munnar Hills
desc: Misty tea-covered hillsides, cool air, and breathtaking valley views in the Western Ghats.

name: Kovalam Beach
desc: A crescent-shaped beach paradise famous for Ayurvedic resorts and spectacular sunsets.

name: Qutub Minar
desc: The world's tallest brick minaret and a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the heart of Delhi.

name: Red Fort
desc: Magnificent Mughal-era fortress of red sandstone and the site of India's Independence Day.

name: Taj Mahal
desc: A symbol of eternal love and one of the Seven Wonders of the World built by Emperor Shah Jahan.

name: Hawa Mahal
desc: The iconic Palace of Winds with 953 intricately carved windows overlooking Jaipur's bazaars.

name: Jaisalmer Fort
desc: A living golden fort rising from the Thar Desert — one of the world's largest fully intact forts.

name: Pushkar Lake
desc: A sacred Hindu pilgrimage site surrounding a tranquil lake with 52 bathing ghats.

name: Valley of Flowers
desc: A UNESCO World Heritage Site blanketed in alpine wildflowers nestled in the Himalayas.

name: Manali
desc: A paradise for trekkers and adventure lovers surrounded by snow-capped Himalayan peaks.

name: Spiti Valley
desc: A cold desert mountain valley with ancient monasteries and dramatic high-altitude landscapes.

name: Rann of Kutch
desc: The world's largest salt desert turning into a magical white expanse under the full moon.
